channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UK Government Considers Reforms to Student Loan System
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has indicated that the government is reviewing ways to make the student loans system fairer. Speaking at Prime Minister's Questions, he blamed the rising costs of loans...

item.title: Two Arrested in Cornwall Over Alleged £6m Packaging Waste Credit Fraud
item.description: Two individuals have been arrested in connection with an investigation into alleged waste packaging fraud and money laundering, according to the Environment Agency (EA). A 47-year-old man and a 47-yea...

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan visit Jordan to support displaced communities
item.description: Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, have arrived in Jordan for a two-day visit focused on humanitarian efforts for communities affected by conflict and displacement. This marks th...

item.title: UK Survey Reveals Household Energy Habits That Often Lead to Arguments
item.description: A recent survey conducted by Uswitch.com highlights the energy habits in UK households that most frequently spark arguments among family members. The study found that leaving lights on in empty rooms ...

item.title: Martin Short mourns the loss of his daughter Katherine at 42
item.description: Canadian actor Martin Short has announced the tragic death of his daughter, Katherine Short, at the age of 42. Katherine, the eldest of three children adopted by Short and his late wife Nancy Dolman, ...

item.title: UK and Spain agree new post-Brexit arrangements for Gibraltar border
item.description: The UK and Spain have reached an agreement allowing Spanish border guards to carry out passport checks on travellers entering Gibraltar. The measures, outlined in a 1,000-page draft treaty, are intend...

item.title: John Lewis closes housebuilding and property management operations to focus on retail
item.description: John Lewis, the UK retailer, has announced it will close its housebuilding division and halt plans to construct 1,000 homes across three sites. Launched in 2020 as a new revenue stream, the housebuild...

item.title: Israeli National Arrested for Operating Illegal Bio-Lab in Las Vegas Airbnb Garage
item.description: In a shocking discovery, an illegal biological laboratory was found inside an Airbnb garage in Las Vegas. The lab, run by Israeli citizen Ori Solomon, was raided by the FBI, leading to the seizure of ...

item.title: Assisted Dying Bill in England and Wales Expected to Fail Before May Deadline
item.description: The controversial Terminally Ill Adults (End of Life) Bill, which seeks to legalise assisted dying in England and Wales, is set to fail due to insufficient parliamentary time. With only six sitting da...

item.title: Labour MPs Call for Change in Leadership After Gorton and Denton Byelection Defeat
item.description: Keir Starmer is facing significant pressure within his own party following a historic defeat in the Gorton and Denton byelection, where Labour lost a 13,000 majority to the Greens. The defeat has inte...

item.title: Man sentenced to life imprisonment for murdering partner’s five-month-old baby in Swansea
item.description: A 29-year-old man, Thomas Morgan, has been sentenced to life imprisonment for the murder of his partner’s five-month-old son, Jensen-Lee Dougal, following a violent assault at the family home in Swans...

item.title: YouTube video presented as an alibi in Natalie McNally murder trial
item.description: During the trial of Stephen McCullagh for the murder of Natalie McNally, the jury was shown a YouTube live stream that prosecutors claim was pre-recorded. McCullagh, 36, initially presented the video ...

item.title: UK Confirms Chagos Islands Transfer Deal Continues Amid US Concerns
item.description: The UK government has clarified that there is no pause in the process of transferring sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius, despite earlier comments by Foreign Office Minister Hamish Falcone...

item.title: Military plane crash in Bolivia kills 15 and injures 30 after banknotes scatter on busy avenue
item.description: A tragic incident unfolded in Bolivia on Friday evening when an Air Force Hercules cargo plane crashed in the city of El Alto, near the capital La Paz. The aircraft, which had departed from Santa Cruz...

item.title: UK Children’s Weekly Pocket Money Rises 8% in 2025, With Regional Differences
item.description: Children across the UK received an above-inflation increase in pocket money in 2025, according to new data from GoHenry, a financial education app designed to help young people manage money. The avera...

item.title: Former Prince Andrew Arrested in UK on Misconduct Allegations
item.description: In a shocking development, former Prince Andrew Windsor has been arrested in the U.K. on suspicion of misconduct in public office. The 66-year-old royal, who has faced significant public scrutiny over...

item.title: Met Police apologise after misidentifying parliamentary Speaker in Mandelson arrest disclosure
item.description: The Metropolitan Police has issued apologies to two senior parliamentary figures after incorrectly disclosing information linked to the arrest of Lord Mandelson. The former Labour peer and British amb...

item.title: Consumers take legal action against FedEx and Ray-Ban over tariff surcharge refunds
item.description: Two retail customers in the United States have filed proposed class-action lawsuits targeting FedEx and EssilorLuxottica (the parent company of Ray-Ban) over refunds related to tariffs imposed by form...

item.title: Police release image of rare boot in effort to identify woman found off Iona coast
item.description: Police in Scotland have released an image of a 'rare' boot in their search to identify a woman whose body was discovered near the coast of Iona on 23 September 2025. The body, which was found about fi...

item.title: Nine Unanswered Questions About Prince Andrew's £12 Million Settlement with Virginia Giuffre
item.description: In February 2022, Prince Andrew reached an out-of-court settlement with Virginia Giuffre, who had accused him of sexual abuse when she was 17. The reported settlement amount, often cited as £12 millio...
